title: Pentacalia disciformis
intro: Pentacalia disciformis, also known as the Pentacalia species, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the Andes of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can be beneficial in treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia disciformis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pentacalia disciformis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Pentacalia disciformis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pentacalia. Pentacalia disciformis is a member of the tribe Senecioneae, which is a group of plants commonly known as ragworts or hawkweeds. Further classification includes its order Asterales and class Magnoliopsida. This plant is native to Mexico.
